Dārul Muṣṭofā Lit-Tajwīd wal-Qur'ānil-Karīm
Nurturing Qur'ān Exploration & Proper Articulation of Words

The Obligation

The Importance of Tajweed & Qur'anic Recitation

📖
The Word of Allah

The Qur'an is the final, preserved revelation — word for word, letter for letter — exactly as it was revealed to the Prophet ﷺ. Reading it correctly is an act of worship.

🎯
Protects the Meaning

Mispronouncing Arabic letters can alter the meaning of entire Qur'anic verses. Tajweed safeguards the sacred text from distortion and preserves its divine message.

⚖️
A Religious Obligation

Scholars of Islam agree that applying Tajweed when reciting the Qur'an is obligatory upon every Muslim. Allah commands: وَرَتِّلِ الْقُرْآنَ تَرْتِيلًا — "Recite the Qur'an with measured recitation." (Al-Muzzammil: 4)

✨
Reward & Beauty

Every letter recited earns ten rewards. The Prophet ﷺ said: even one who recites the Qur'an while struggling through it shall have two rewards — for the recitation and for the effort.

✦

Beginners' Guide

وَرَتِّلِ الْقُرْآنَ تَرْتِيلًا

Al-Muzzammil: 4 — "Recite the Qur'an with measured recitation"

The Benefits of Learning Tajweed & the Qur'an

01

Preservation of the Divine Word

The Qur'an has been preserved word for word, letter for letter, across fourteen centuries. Tajweed ensures this preservation continues through every individual recitation, exactly as the Prophet ﷺ received it.

02

Protection of Meaning

Arabic is a precise language — a single change in pronunciation can alter the meaning of a verse entirely. Tajweed protects the meaning of the Qur'an from distortion, keeping the message of Allah intact.

03

Fulfilment of a Religious Obligation

Islamic scholars agree that applying Tajweed in Qur'anic recitation is obligatory (Fard) upon every Muslim. Learning it is not optional — it is an act of obedience to the command of Allah Himself.

04

Ten Rewards Per Letter

The Prophet ﷺ confirmed: every single letter of the Qur'an recited earns ten rewards. For one who recites while striving and struggling, there are two rewards — one for the recitation and one for the effort.

05

Beautifies Recitation & Elevates Prayer

Proper Tajweed brings melody, rhythm, and beauty to the recitation. It elevates the quality of every Salah and transforms daily worship from routine repetition into a deeply meaningful spiritual experience.

06

A Lifelong Companion

The Prophet ﷺ said: "The best among you are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it." (Sahih al-Bukhari). A reciter who knows Tajweed carries a lifelong treasure that benefits them in this world and the next.

📖 What You Will Learn in This Series

Three core skills that build the foundation of correct Qur'an recitation

م
Makhārijمَخَارِج

The exact place in the mouth or throat where each Arabic letter is pronounced from. You'll learn to pronounce every letter correctly, right from its true point of articulation.

ص
Ṣifātصِفَات

The special qualities that give each Arabic letter its unique sound. You'll learn how to give every letter its correct, distinct pronunciation.

ح
Ḥurūfحُرُوف

The Arabic letters used to read and recite the Qur'an. You'll learn to recognize, pronounce, and read them accurately — the foundation everything else is built on.
